1	= Spring Boot image:https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/actions/workflows/build-and-deploy-snapshot.yml/badge.svg?branch=main["Build Status", link="https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/actions/workflows/build-and-deploy-snapshot.yml?query=branch%3Amain"] image:https://img.shields.io/badge/Revved%20up%20by-Develocity-06A0CE?logo=Gradle&labelColor=02303A["Revved up by Develocity", link="https://ge.spring.io/scans?&search.rootProjectNames=Spring%20Boot%20Build&search.rootProjectNames=spring-boot-build"]
2	
3	:docs: https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot
4	:github: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot
5	
6	Spring Boot helps you to create Spring-powered, production-grade applications and services with absolute minimum fuss.
7	It takes an opinionated view of the Spring platform so that new and existing users can quickly get to the bits they need.
8	
9	You can use Spring Boot to create stand-alone Java applications that can be started using `java -jar` or more traditional WAR deployments.
10	We also provide a command-line tool that runs Spring scripts.
11	
12	Our primary goals are:
13	
14	* Provide a radically faster and widely accessible getting started experience for all Spring development.
15	* Be opinionated, but get out of the way quickly as requirements start to diverge from the defaults.
16	* Provide a range of non-functional features common to large classes of projects (for example, embedded servers, security, metrics, health checks, externalized configuration).
17	* Absolutely no code generation and no requirement for XML configuration.
18	
19	
20	
21	== Installation and Getting Started
22	
23	The {docs}[reference documentation] includes detailed {docs}/installing.html[installation instructions] as well as a comprehensive {docs}/tutorial/first-application/index.html[``getting started``] guide.
24	
25	Here is a quick teaser of a complete Spring Boot application in Java:
26	
27	[source,java]
28	----
29	import org.springframework.boot.*;
30	import org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.*;
31	import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.*;
32	
33	@RestController
34	@SpringBootApplication
35	public class Example {
36	
37		@RequestMapping("/")
38		String home() {
39			return "Hello World!";
40		}
41	
42		public static void main(String[] args) {
43			SpringApplication.run(Example.class, args);
44		}
45	
46	}
47	----
48	
49	
50	
51	== Getting Help
52	
53	Are you having trouble with Spring Boot? We want to help!
54	
55	* Check the {docs}/[reference documentation], especially the {docs}/how-to/index.html[How-to's] -- they provide solutions to the most common questions.
56	* Learn the Spring basics -- Spring Boot builds on many other Spring projects; check the https://spring.io[spring.io] website for a wealth of reference documentation.
57	  If you are new to Spring, try one of the https://spring.io/guides[guides].
58	* If you are upgrading, read the {github}/wiki[release notes] for upgrade instructions and "new and noteworthy" features.
59	* Ask a question -- we monitor https://stackoverflow.com[stackoverflow.com] for questions tagged with https://stackoverflow.com/tags/spring-boot[`spring-boot`].
60	* Report bugs with Spring Boot at {github}/issues[github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/issues].
61	
62	
63	
64	== Contributing
65	
66	We welcome contributions of all kinds!
67	Please read our link:CONTRIBUTING.adoc[contribution guidelines] before submitting a pull request.
68	
69	
70	
71	== Reporting Issues
72	
73	Spring Boot uses GitHub's integrated issue tracking system to record bugs and feature requests.
74	If you want to raise an issue, please follow the recommendations below:
75	
76	* Before you log a bug, please search the {github}/issues[issue tracker] to see if someone has already reported the problem.
77	* If the issue doesn't already exist, {github}/issues/new[create a new issue].
78	* Please provide as much information as possible with the issue report.
79	We like to know the Spring Boot version, operating system, and JVM version you're using.
80	* If you need to paste code or include a stack trace, use Markdown.
81	+++```+++ escapes before and after your text.
82	* If possible, try to create a test case or project that replicates the problem and attach it to the issue.
83	
84	
85	
86	== Building from Source
87	
88	You don't need to build from source to use Spring Boot.
89	If you want to try out the latest and greatest, Spring Boot can be built and published to your local Maven cache using the https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/gradle_wrapper.html[Gradle wrapper].
90	You also need JDK 25.
91	
92	[source,shell]
93	----
94	$ ./gradlew publishToMavenLocal
95	----
96	
97	This command builds all modules and publishes them to your local Maven cache.
98	It won't run any of the tests.
99	If you want to build everything, use the `build` task:
100	
101	[source,shell]
102	----
103	$ ./gradlew build
104	----
105	
106	
107	
108	== Guides
109	
110	The https://spring.io/[spring.io] site contains several guides that show how to use Spring Boot step-by-step:
111	
112	* https://spring.io/guides/gs/spring-boot/[Building an Application with Spring Boot] is an introductory guide that shows you how to create an application, run it, and add some management services.
113	* https://spring.io/guides/gs/actuator-service/[Building a RESTful Web Service with Spring Boot Actuator] is a guide to creating a REST web service and also shows how the server can be configured.
114	
115	
116	
117	== License
118	
119	Spring Boot is Open Source software released under the https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html[Apache 2.0 license].

Auto-configuration classes are usually 37 * applied based on your classpath and what beans you have defined. 38 *

39 * When using {@link SpringBootApplication @SpringBootApplication}, the auto-configuration 40 * of the context is automatically enabled and adding this annotation has therefore no 41 * additional effect. 42 *

43 * Auto-configuration tries to be as intelligent as possible and will back-away as you 44 * define more of your own configuration. You can always manually {@link #exclude()} any 45 * configuration that you never want to apply (use {@link #excludeName()} if you don't 46 * have access to them). You can also exclude them through the 47 * {@code spring.autoconfigure.exclude} property. Auto-configuration is always applied 48 * after user-defined beans have been registered. 49 *

50 * The package of the class that is annotated with {@code @EnableAutoConfiguration}, 51 * usually through {@code @SpringBootApplication}, has specific significance and is often 52 * used as a 'default'. For example, it will be used when scanning for {@code @Entity} 53 * classes. It is generally recommended that you place {@code @EnableAutoConfiguration} 54 * (if you're not using {@code @SpringBootApplication}) in a root package so that all 55 * sub-packages and classes can be searched. 56 *

57 * Auto-configuration classes are regular Spring {@link Configuration @Configuration} 58 * beans. They are located using {@link ImportCandidates}. Generally auto-configuration 59 * beans are {@link Conditional @Conditional} beans (most often using 60 * {@link ConditionalOnClass @ConditionalOnClass} and 61 * {@link ConditionalOnMissingBean @ConditionalOnMissingBean} annotations). 62 * 63 * @author Phillip Webb 64 * @author Stephane Nicoll 65 * @since 1.0.0 66 * @see ConditionalOnBean 67 * @see ConditionalOnMissingBean 68 * @see ConditionalOnClass 69 * @see AutoConfigureAfter 70 * @see SpringBootApplication 71 */ 72 @Target(ElementType.TYPE) 73 @Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME) 74 @Documented 75 @Inherited 76 @AutoConfigurationPackage 77 @Import(AutoConfigurationImportSelector.class) 78 public @interface EnableAutoConfiguration { 79 80 /** 81 * Environment property that can be used to override when auto-configuration is 82 * enabled. 83 */ 84 String ENABLED_OVERRIDE_PROPERTY = "spring.boot.enableautoconfiguration"; 85 86 /** 87 * Exclude specific auto-configuration classes such that they will never be applied. 88 *

89 * Since this annotation is parsed by loading class bytecode, it is safe to specify 90 * classes here that may ultimately not be on the classpath, but only if this 91 * annotation is directly on the affected component and not if this annotation 92 * is used as a composed, meta-annotation. In order to use this annotation as a 93 * meta-annotation, only use the {@link #excludeName()} attribute. 94 * @return the classes to exclude 95 */ 96 Class[] exclude() default {}; 97 98 /** 99 * Exclude specific auto-configuration class names such that they will never be 100 * applied. 101 * @return the class names to exclude 102 * @since 1.3.0 103 */ 104 String[] excludeName() default {}; 105 106 } 107

This interface is designed to allow fast removal 29 * of auto-configuration classes before their bytecode is even read. 30 *

31 * An {@link AutoConfigurationImportFilter} may implement any of the following 32 * {@link org.springframework.beans.factory.Aware Aware} interfaces, and their respective 33 * methods will be called prior to {@link #match}: 34 *

    35 *
  • {@link EnvironmentAware}
    • 36 *
  • {@link BeanFactoryAware}
    • 37 *
  • {@link BeanClassLoaderAware}
    • 38 *
  • {@link ResourceLoaderAware}
    • 39 *
40 * 41 * @author Phillip Webb 42 * @since 1.5.0 43 */ 44 @FunctionalInterface 45 public interface AutoConfigurationImportFilter { 46 47 /** 48 * Apply the filter to the given auto-configuration class candidates. 49 * @param autoConfigurationClasses the auto-configuration classes being considered. 50 * This array may contain {@code null} elements. Implementations should not change the 51 * values in this array. 52 * @param autoConfigurationMetadata access to the meta-data generated by the 53 * auto-configure annotation processor 54 * @return a boolean array indicating which of the auto-configuration classes should 55 * be imported. The returned array must be the same size as the incoming 56 * {@code autoConfigurationClasses} parameter. Entries containing {@code false} will 57 * not be imported. 58 */ 59 boolean[] match(@Nullable String[] autoConfigurationClasses, AutoConfigurationMetadata autoConfigurationMetadata); 60 61 } 62
